body.text:
The public demo should prove that a personal site can read serious Org, not only
three TODO rows.  This source intentionally mixes prose, tables, links, drawers,
timestamps, source blocks, lists, footnotes, and planning metadata.

The key rule is simple: TypeScript may arrange the experience, but native Org
syntax remains the source of truth.[fn:native-org]

- *Blog* headings still become articles when tagged =:blog:=.
- /Italic/, *bold*, _underline_, +strike-through+, =verbatim=, and ~code~ should
  survive rendering.
- Links can point to [[https://orgmode.org/][Org mode]], [[id:syntax-table-record][stable records]],
  and [[attachment:syntax-map.png][attachment-like evidence]].
- Footnotes, tables, drawers, and source blocks should not flatten into plain
  text.

#+BEGIN_QUOTE
Knowledge is useful only when it can be revisited, re-ranked, and acted on.
#+END_QUOTE
[fn:native-org] In this demo that means property drawers, planning lines,

headline tags, source blocks, and rendered HTML all come through the WASM parser
boundary instead of a TypeScript approximation.

This article doubles as an agenda item.  The same heading must appear in the
Blog reader, the Notes list, and the Agenda workbench.

1. Inline emphasis: *bold*, /italic/, _underline_, +deleted+, =verbatim=,
   ~inline-code~.
2. Nested list:
   - Parser-owned facts
     - TODO keyword
     - priority cookie
     - planning line
   - Frontend-owned presentation
     - source cards
     - article library
     - agenda panels
3. Checkbox state:
   - [X] render checked task
   - [-] render partial task
   - [ ] render open task

#+BEGIN_EXAMPLE
This example block should remain monospaced.
It is useful for command transcripts and raw Org fragments.
#+END_EXAMPLE

| Syntax family | Example                    | Frontend risk                  |
|---------------+----------------------------+--------------------------------|
| Planning      | DEADLINE / SCHEDULED       | hidden from agenda grouping    |
| Properties    | =:AREA:=, =:EFFORT:=, =:ID:= | collapsed into plain text      |
| Links         | web, id, attachment        | lost labels or wrong target    |
| Blocks        | quote, example, source     | broken spacing or overflow     |
| Tables        | org table                  | unreadable in Zen mode         |

This record exists to verify drawer preservation and display.  It should expose
multiple key-value pairs without requiring the user to inspect raw Org.

The frontend should show why this fact is current: TODO state, schedule,
stable identity, habit cadence, links, and lifecycle notes all remain visible.
See [[id:property-drawer-coverage][property drawer coverage]] for the source
record that seeded this contract.

body.text:
This older layout is intentionally retained as historical evidence so the UI
can explain stale and superseded candidates instead of hiding them.

Archived records should stay inspectable while remaining suppressed for active
agent decisions.

The body carries both an attachment link and an ID link:
[[attachment:syntax-map.png][syntax map]] and [[id:syntax-rendering-checklist][rendering checklist]].
